meta.title: Mamdani administration personnel tracker
meta.departuresNote: Departures focus on officials who served into the Mamdani administration and then left — holdovers who resigned and any Mamdani appointees who departed. A pure end-of-Adams exit is not counted as a Mamdani-administration departure.
meta.note: Senior and mid-level political (non-civil-service) appointees brought into City Hall and the agencies since the start of the Mamdani administration, plus those who have departed. Excludes rank-and-file civil servants (uniformed police and fire, sanitation workers, public school teachers, and similar).

salaryReference.source: NYC Quadrennial Advisory Commission, Select New York City Mayoral Appointees base-salary schedule

departed.name: Molly Wasow Park
departed.title: Commissioner, Department of Social Services
departed.detail: Adams-era holdover who oversaw homeless services and the Human Resources Administration. Resigned after weeks of uncertainty about her role, saying the department needs a permanent leader; later named CEO of the NYC Housing Partnership. Succeeded by Erin Dalton.

departed.name: Jocelyn Strauber
departed.title: Commissioner, Department of Investigation
departed.detail: Adams-era holdover and the first DOI commissioner to run a corruption investigation that indicted the sitting mayor. Said there was no prompt path to staying on permanently. Succeeded by Nadia Shihata.

departed.name: Jess Dannhauser
departed.title: Commissioner, Administration for Children's Services
departed.detail: Adams-era holdover. Submitted resignation roughly a week after inauguration, departing by early March. Succeeded by Rebecca Jones Gaston.

departed.name: Catherine Almonte Da Costa
departed.title: Director of Appointments
departed.detail: Resigned roughly one day after being named, after antisemitic posts from 2011-2012 resurfaced. Part of the transition before Inauguration Day.

departed.name: Robert Tucker
departed.title: Commissioner, Fire Department
departed.detail: Adams-era FDNY commissioner who resigned citing fundamental differences with the incoming mayor. Effective Dec. 19, 2025; succeeded by Lillian Bonsignore.

departed.name: About 179 Adams-era staffers
departed.title: Mass resignation request (transition)
departed.detail: Context entry, not a single person. The incoming administration asked roughly 179 holdover staffers to formally resign before the term began; reporting described close to 200 employees leaving in the first days. Lower-level and individually unnamed departures are represented here rather than listed one by one.
